summary: Soundboard recording with crisp mix (minor drum digi‑noise) captures one of the most celebrated 1991 Giants Stadium shows – Eyes opener, nonstop Dark Star teases and legendary Loose Lucy/May As Well jams.
review: The show is repeatedly called a “must‑have” – an explosive Eyes of the World opening, relentless Dark Star motifs, and career‑high moments from Loose Lucy, Might As Well, St. of Circumstance and the New Speedway/Truckin’ sequence. The SBD source is generally clean and well balanced; a few reviewers note a brief digi‑noise burst at the start of the drums and missing the first couple seconds of Eyes in some copies, but overall the audio quality is very good.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80 reviews overwhelmingly rate the performance as exceptional (most 5‑star) and praise the soundboard source; only isolated audio glitches keep the recording rating just below “excellent.”

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with good stereo separation. A few copies miss the first seconds of Eyes; a short digi‑noise burst on drums at the very start is noted by one reviewer.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Seamless transitions from the thunder‑charged Eyes opener through a Dark Star‑tinged first set, then a second‑set marathon of high‑energy jams (New Speedway/Truckin', UJB, St. of Circumstance) that keeps the momentum without any dead spots.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as “at his best” and guitar tone praised; several reviewers note his energetic soloing and charismatic stage presence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally tight bass work, though a few reviewers felt Phil was less prominent in the mix on this copy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brown Eyed Women and Loose Lucy highlighted for strong rhythm‑guitar and vocal contributions; many cite his interplay with Jerry as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce (keyboards) credited with driving the show, injecting Dark Star riffs throughout; Vince’s synth work gets mixed reactions – some love the “going nuts” vibe, others find it intrusive.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey praised for powerful playing; one reviewer mentions a brief loud digi‑noise burst on the drums at the start.
